It seems we have updated the configuration on our end to Ed Zhao last fall as requested,  but they have not updated the DESCRIPTION accordingly.  Please remember as we switch maintainer access, if the correspondence should also be updated that needs to be updated in the DESCRITPION of the package by the newly accessible maintainer with a valid version bump. we will always try to contact the maintainer designated in the DESCRIPTION for auto notifications and as we reach out with anything from the core team directly.

Please let us know if Senbai should also have access.  We will have to reach out to create a BiocCredentials account for Senbai as I do not see one already existing.

Cheers


Lori Shepherd - Kern

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Community has moved from Slack to Zulip
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Community has moved from Slack to Zulip
messages.body_plain:
Apologies if you receive this message more than once ? we?re sending it to both Slack users and the bioc-devel list to ensure everyone is informed.

Dear Bioconductor community,

As part of our transition from Slack, we?ve now set up our new community space on Zulip:
URL: https://community-bioc.zulipchat.com

If you had an account on Bioconductor Slack, you should have received an email with the  subject "Password reset request for community-bioc", inviting you to log in. If you didn?t receive it ? or if your institution flagged it as phishing ? you can safely go to the link above and use ?Forgot your password?? with the email you used on Slack to get started.

For more details on the move and what to expect, see our blog post:
https://blog.bioconductor.org/posts/2025-05-29-slack-to-zulip

We look forward to seeing you there!

Best,
Maria

Maria Doyle, PhD
Bioconductor Community Manager

School of Medicine,
University of Limerick, Limerick, V94 T9PX
Ireland
Email: maria.doyle at ul.ie<mailto:maria.doyle at ul.ie>
Phone (office): +353 61 234 768
Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

  *   GBCC 2025 (June 23-26, New York, USA)<https://gbcc2025.org/>
  *   EuroBioC 2025 (September 17-19, Barcelona, Spain)<https://eurobioc2025.bioconductor.org/>

I work flexible hours across several time zones. I don't expect you to read or respond to my emails outside of your normal working hours.

messages.body_plain:
Hi,

I've had an ongoing error for quite some time with karyoploteR and I fixed it a few weeks ago. However, it seems that the builder is not picking the newest version.

The current version in RELESASE_3_21 is 1.34.2 (and this is what I see in DESCRIPTION if I clone the repo) but the builder is picking 1.34.0 and so the reports keeps failing. Any idea of what's going on? If it can be of any help, the package was failing during release, so I think the version being served to users is 1.33.0

The devel servers are picking the newest version 1.35.2, but they are failing there because of bamsignals.

Thanks a lot for your help!

Bernat

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] basilisk falling back to pip and breaking environment setup
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] basilisk falling back to pip and breaking environment setup
messages.body_plain:
Hi basilisk team,

First, thank you for your continued work on basilisk and for the fixes and improvements in recent versions. Also thanks to Vincent for the helpful replies to my previous questions.

I?ve recently run into an issue where basilisk seems to fall back to using pip (via reticulate:::pip_install(python, packages)) instead of setting up the environment with conda, even when all packages are available via conda. This is happening in packages like scifer, MOFA2, and pirat, which previously built fine. Now, builds fail with errors when trying to install packages that are supposed to install via conda but fall on pip.

The basilisk environment definitions still specify conda packages and channels (bioconda, conda-forge), and nothing has changed there. Has something changed in how basilisk sets up environments or handles fallbacks?

Would appreciate any guidance on how to make sure conda is used as expecte


Best,
Rodrigo

--
Rodrigo Arcoverde Cerveira
PhD student in Karolinska Institutet, Sweden
Karin Lor?s research group
CMM L8:00, Visionsgatan 18

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel]  basilisk falling back to pip and breaking environment setup
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel]  basilisk falling back to pip and breaking environment setup
messages.body_plain:
Basilisk recently switched to a conda-less approach to setting up Python
environments, see discussion in:

- https://github.com/LTLA/basilisk/pull/49, you should have been mentioned
in the list of affected packages
- https://github.com/LTLA/basilisk/pull/52, which provides some guidance on
conda-only packages
- https://github.com/LTLA/basilisk/issues/48, for an overview of the
motivation
- https://github.com/LTLA/basilisk/issues/20, for a typical GLIBCXX error

As mentioned in #52, if your required Python dependencies are only present
in conda (igblast seems to be the main culprit), you can use the repurposed
basilisk.utils package to auto-provision a conda environment for you.

That said: whether this conda environment can be used successfully with
reticulate is another matter. It seems that, with the passage of time, the
conda binaries will increasingly depend on more recent versions of system
libraries. This will result in a greater rate of linker failures when
attempting to use reticulate to load the associated Python packages into an
R session linked against older versions of those libraries. It's probably
safest to just run the conda tools via system2, like what some packages do
for Python 2 environments (which aren't even supported by reticulate
anymore).

-A


On Wed, Jun 11, 2025 at 2:48?AM Rodrigo Arcoverde Cerveira <
rodrigo.arcoverdi at gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi basilisk team,
>
> First, thank you for your continued work on basilisk and for the fixes and
> improvements in recent versions. Also thanks to Vincent for the helpful
> replies to my previous questions.
>
> I?ve recently run into an issue where basilisk seems to fall back to using
> pip (via reticulate:::pip_install(python, packages)) instead of setting up
> the environment with conda, even when all packages are available via conda.
> This is happening in packages like scifer, MOFA2, and pirat, which
> previously built fine. Now, builds fail with errors when trying to install
> packages that are supposed to install via conda but fall on pip.
>
> The basilisk environment definitions still specify conda packages and
> channels (bioconda, conda-forge), and nothing has changed there. Has
> something changed in how basilisk sets up environments or handles fallbacks?
>
> Would appreciate any guidance on how to make sure conda is used as expecte
>
>
> Best,
> Rodrigo
>
> --
> Rodrigo Arcoverde Cerveira
> PhD student in Karolinska Institutet, Sweden
> Karin Lor??s research group
> CMM L8:00, Visionsgatan 18
>
>

Greetings,

I would like to update the data in our PhyloProfileData package. I have updated the raw data and also made changes to the make-data.R file which generates the .RData files for the package. However, after the latest build, the data remains unchanged, even after reinstalling R and all related packages. messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Help updating data in ExperimentHub
messages.body_plain:
So a few follow up questions and comments

It seems like you a referencing an ExperimentHub data package with the snapshot date;  is that correct?

And if so, you mentioned you updated the raw data;  you have updated those files on the hosted server?  And did you request an update to the metadata in the experiment hub database.  The data files would need to be updated in order to have the correct download and access.

The update will only be available using Bioconductor devel 3.22;  a snapshot of  4-12-2025 is for release not devel so you would have to use devel in order to see the change.   It is possible to update release but it is not recommended for consistency and to keep release as stable and reproducible for users as possible.

If you need further assistance with updating the experiment hub data;   I suggest reaching out to hubs at bioconductor.org

Cheers,




Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.body_plain:
Dear Lori,

Thanks for your quick reply! 

Yes, I was referring to the snapshot date of the PhyloProfileData data package. I have updated the raw data on the hosted server. In the inst/scripts/ directory of the data package I have two files make-data.R and make-metadata.R, which generate the .RData files and the metadata.csv, respectively. I will try updating the data version in the metadata file and submit again. Currently, neither the devel nor the release version reflexes the changes. 

I understand the importance of consistency and reproducibility. However, this data package is used solely as a demo data for the Bioconductor PhyloProfile package. In order to fully demonstrate the feature of the current version of PhyloProfile, we need to update the data, even in the release version.

Many thanks again for your support!

Best,
Vinh

messages.body_plain:
Hi,

I received the notice below about gDNAx devel failing in linux, here's 
the report:

https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/gDNAx/nebbiolo2-buildsrc.html

the error seems to have something to do with loading data from the cache 
of the ExperimentHub when building the vignette. It does not happen in 
linux-aarch64 or in any other platform, and in release gives a similar 
error, but on the Windows platform:

https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.21/bioc-LATEST/gDNAx/palomino7-buildsrc.html

while for linux and every other platform builds fine. This has been 
happening since we switched to the new release, I tried to bump the 
version in both, devel and release, but the error in these two specific 
platforms remains. Could we try to reset somehow the ExperimentHub cache 
in these two platforms for gDNAx? of course, if you have any other 
suggestion that I can try, please let me know.

thanks!

robert.

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] EuroBioC2025: Late-Breaking Poster Call Now Open
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor community,

We are now accepting late-breaking poster abstracts for EuroBioC2025, taking place in Barcelona, September 17?19, 2025.

If you missed the initial submission round, this is a great opportunity to present your work and connect with the Bioconductor community.


  *   Submission deadline: Friday, July 11, 2025
  *   Submission form: https://forms.gle/ewTGdKzB2xyEg58f8
  *   Notification of acceptance: by July 15
  *   Optional short poster pitch during the conference

Topics of interest include (but are not limited to):


  *   Genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics
  *   Multiomics and data integration
  *   Infrastructure, visualisation, benchmarking
  *   Statistical methods and developer tools

More information: https://eurobioc2025.bioconductor.org

Please feel free to share with colleagues or collaborators who may be interested.

Best regards,
Maria (on behalf of the EuroBioC2025 organising team)

Maria Doyle, PhD
Bioconductor Community Manager

School of Medicine,
University of Limerick, Limerick, V94 T9PX
Ireland
Email: maria.doyle at ul.ie<mailto:maria.doyle at ul.ie>
Phone (office): +353 61 234 768
Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Switch from GenomeInfoDb to new Seqinfo package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Switch from GenomeInfoDb to new Seqinfo package
messages.body_plain:
Dear fellow Bioconductor developers,

The Seqinfo class and its accessors were moved from the GenomeInfoDb 
package to the new Seqinfo package in BioC 3.22. Packages that import 
any of the accessors 'seqnames', 'seqlevels', 'seqlenghs', 'isCircular', 
'genome', 'seqinfo', and/or the 'Seqinfo' constructor function from 
GenomeInfoDb will now need to import them from the Seqinfo package. The 
main remaining business of the GenomeInfoDb package is the 
"seqlevelsStyle business" which has grown significantly in complexity in 
the recent years. Having the "core Seqinfo business" (i.e. Seqinfo class 
+ constructor + accessors) in its own lightweight package with a reduced 
number of deps makes a lot of sense.

I've switched 40+ packages from GenomeInfoDb to Seqinfo already, but 
many more packages will need to make that switch. Note that I committed 
and pushed directly to git.bioconductor.org to expedite things, so 
please make sure to resync your GitHub repo with the repo at 
git.bioconductor.org if your package depends on GenomeInfoDb because 
there's a chance that it has been switched.

A couple more things:

- The latest version of GenomeInfoDb (1.45.5) imports the new Seqinfo 
package and re-exports the Seqinfo constructor and accessors. This is to 
help with backward compatibility.
- Even with the re-export trick, some packages will still fail. I 
actually expect a significant number of packages to fail on Monday when 
the next daily build report for BioC 3.22 is out. I'll try to take care 
of as many as I can but it might take a few days before I start doing 
so. Depending on how many there are, I might also need help.

Sorry in advance for the inconvenience and thanks for your patience.

H.

-- 
Herv? Pag?s

Bioconductor Core Team
hpages.on.github at gmail.com

Can Ge or an admin push it?

Have a nice day,

Charles

messages.body_plain:
We hope that the maintainer is still active and willing to maintain their
package;  Bioconductor admin will not push for a maintainer to ensure all
packages are actively maintained.  If we do not hear from the maintainer or
have an explicit request to deprecate the package from them,  we can have
someone in the community volunteer to maintain the package consistent with
Bioconductors end of life policy and package naming policy that maintainers
agree to on submission; but that is only if the current maintainer does not
respond and most ideally with the current maintainers permission. Let us
see if Ge Tan responds shortly.


Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

Sorry not monitoring this email for some time. I no longer have the time to maintain the CNEr package and would like to deprecate it unless there is some volunteer to keep maintaining it.

Thanks,
Ge

From: Lori Shepherd <shepherd.bioc at gmail.com>
Date: Monday, 23 June 2025 at 13:05
To: Charles Plessy <charles.plessy at oist.jp>
Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>, Ge Tan <ge_tan at live.com>, gtan at me.com <gtan at me.com>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] CNEr fails to build; patch available.

We hope that the maintainer is still active and willing to maintain their package;  Bioconductor admin will not push for a maintainer to ensure all packages are actively maintained.  If we do not hear from the maintainer or have an explicit request to deprecate the package from them,  we can have someone in the community volunteer to maintain the package consistent with Bioconductors end of life policy and package naming policy that maintainers agree to on submission; but that is only if the current maintainer does not respond and most ideally with the current maintainers permission. Let us see if Ge Tan responds shortly.



Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center


On Sun, Jun 22, 2025 at 9:22?PM Charles Plessy <charles.plessy at oist.jp<mailto:charles.plessy at oist.jp>> wrote:
Hi Ge and Bioc admins,

CNEr fails to build with recent GCC, preventing the installation of its
dependency chain.

     https://github.com/ge11232002/CNEr/issues/28

A patch is available from NixOS. It looks sane to me.


https://patch-diff.githubusercontent.com/raw/NixOS/nixpkgs/pull/411384.patch

Can Ge or an admin push it?

Have a nice day,

Charles

messages.body_plain:
Thanks Ge for the answer, and for all your work on CNEr.

Me and some of my coworkers are still using your package, or at least a subset
of its functions, typically after aligning genomes externally with the
nf-core/pairgenomealign pipeline, which can output Axt format (CNEr input).

    https://nf-co.re/pairgenomealign

Actually, I also wrote an informal package that can load MAF or GFF from the
same pipeline, and it would be straightforward to write a function that
reads these files into CNEr::Axt objects.

    https://oist.github.io/GenomicBreaks/

Based on this, if nobody volunteers to maintain CNEr to its full extent, I
would be happy to help to maintain a streamlined version that does not rely on
compiled C code (a frequent source of bitrot) and does not support running
external aligners (since there are now pipelines that can do it easily). 

Have a nice day,

Charles

messages.body_plain:
Hi Ge,

Thank you for your email, and thank you, Charles, for bringing it to our
attention. Since CNEr was originally developed in the Lenhard group, and
the members of the Lenhard group, as well as mine, use it regularly, in
consultation with Boris Lenhard (cc'd), we decided it would be beneficial
for both the Lenhard group and mine to continue maintaining and
developing it further.

Can Boris and I be maintainers for now, so we can promptly react when
needed, until we discuss who will take the active maintenance role in the
following weeks?

Thank you in advance.

Kind regards,
Damir Baranasic
